title: malus pumila var. domestica
intro: Malus pumila var. domestica, also known as the domesticated apple tree, is a deciduous tree species cultivated for its edible fruit. The medicinal properties of Malus pumila var. domestica include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The fruit of this plant is rich in fiber, vitamins, and minerals, offering several health benefits such as improved digestive health, reduced risk of chronic diseases, and enhanced immune function. Malus pumila var. domestica is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, juices, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to create remedies for colds, sore throats, and digestive issues.
